description:
You are a localization engineer who just received two urgent Spanish‐language fixes for the in-house translation database.  
Use only the standard SQLite CLI tool (`sqlite3`) to perform the updates.

Current project layout  
/home/user/projects/loc/db/translations.db   ← SQLite database  
/home/user/projects/loc/report/              ← directory for human-readable reports  

Database schema (table already exists):  
CREATE TABLE strings(  
    id    INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,  
    key   TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,  
    en    TEXT NOT NULL,  
    es    TEXT  
);

Your two required changes  
1. Insert a brand-new key:  
   key = reset_password  
   en  = Reset your password.  
   es  = Restablece tu contraseña.  

2. Update the existing key goodbye_msg so that its Spanish string becomes:  
   es = Adiós.  

After you complete the work, generate a small plain-text report file so that QA can immediately see what you did.  The report must be saved exactly as  
/home/user/projects/loc/report/translation_update_report.txt  

Required report format (exactly two lines, no extra spaces or blank lines; each line ends with a single newline “\n” character):  
INSERTED: reset_password  
UPDATED: goodbye_msg  

Automated grading will verify:  
• The database file still exists and is a valid SQLite database.  
• Table strings now contains a row whose key is reset_password, English text matches exactly “Reset your password.”, and Spanish text matches exactly “Restablece tu contraseña.”.  
• The row whose key is goodbye_msg has its Spanish (es) column updated to exactly “Adiós.” while other columns remain unchanged.  
• The report file exists at the precise path given and its contents match the required format byte-for-byte.

No other output, files, or schema changes are expected.
truth:
Pre-existing state before the agent starts:

Directory structure already present  
/home/user/projects/loc/db/  (0700 user:user)  
/home/user/projects/loc/report/  (0700 user:user)

SQLite database preloaded at /home/user/projects/loc/db/translations.db  
-- Contents of table strings --
id | key           | en                     | es
---+---------------+------------------------+----
1  | hello_msg     | Hello!                 | ¡Hola!
2  | goodbye_msg   | Goodbye!               |        (NULL)

Important: id values are deterministic because AUTOINCREMENT starts at 1 and only the two rows above exist.  When the agent inserts the new key reset_password it must receive id = 3.  After the agent updates goodbye_msg, its id must stay 2 and only the es column changes from NULL to “Adiós.”.

Ground-truth after successful completion:

1. A new row (id=3) in strings
   key = reset_password
   en  = Reset your password.
   es  = Restablece tu contraseña.

2. Row with id=2 (key goodbye_msg) now has
   es = Adiós.

3. /home/user/projects/loc/report/translation_update_report.txt exists, permissions 0644.
   File content exactly (including final newline):
     INSERTED: reset_password\n
     UPDATED: goodbye_msg\n
name: task_000000_73deeaea
